steps.title: Sign in to Azure portal
steps.instruction: Sign in to the Azure portal - https://portal.azure.com. To proceed to the portal, select Cancel on the Welcome to Azure splash screen.
steps.aiResponse:
I can't execute this browser-based lab step directly. I don't have Playwright MCP or browser control capabilities in this CLI session.

This step needs to be run through the **lab-drift-runner pipeline**, which uses the Copilot SDK + Playwright MCP to control a browser. You can execute it with:

```bash
npm run scan -- <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

Or if you want me to help with something else related to this step — like reviewing the spec, modifying the runner code, or analyzing existing drift reports — I'm happy to help with that.

driftIssues.instructionText: Sign in to the Azure portal - https://portal.azure.com. To proceed to the portal, select Cancel on the Welcome to Azure splash screen.
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID
steps.instruction: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID. Select the Overview blade and then the Manage tenants tab. Return to the Entra ID page by pressing back in the browser or selecting the option in the breadcrumb menu.
steps.aiResponse:
As I mentioned, I cannot execute browser-based lab steps. I don't have Playwright MCP or browser control — I can't navigate to Azure portal, click UI elements, or take screenshots.

These lab steps are designed to be executed by the **lab-drift-runner pipeline** (`runner-mcp-drift.ts`), which uses the Copilot SDK with Playwright MCP for browser automation.

**To run these steps**, use:
```bash
npm run scan -- <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

**What I *can* help with:**
- Modifying runner/drift detection code
- Reviewing or generating lab specs
- Analyzing existing drift reports
- Debugging pipeline issues
- Any code/config changes in this repo

Would you like help with any of those instead?

driftIssues.instructionText: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID. Select the Overview blade and then the Manage tenants tab. Return to the Entra ID page by pressing back in the browser or selecting the option in the breadcrumb menu.
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Access Users management section
steps.instruction: In the Manage blade, select Users; then in the New user drop-down select Create new user.
steps.aiResponse:
I understand you're sending me sequential lab steps, but my answer remains the same: **I cannot execute browser-based steps.** I have no browser control, no Playwright MCP, and no ability to interact with the Azure portal UI.

It looks like these steps are being dispatched to me as if I were the AI agent inside `runner-mcp-drift.ts`. I'm not — I'm a CLI/code assistant.

**If you're trying to debug why the runner isn't executing these steps**, I can help investigate the pipeline code. Want me to look into how `runner-mcp-drift.ts` dispatches steps to the Copilot SDK session?

driftIssues.instructionText: In the Manage blade, select Users; then in the New user drop-down select Create new user.
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Create internal user az104-user1
steps.instruction: Create a new user with the following settings: User principal name: az104-user1, Display name: az104-user1, Auto-generate password: checked, Account enabled: checked. On the Properties tab set Job title: IT Lab Administrator, Department: IT, Usage location: United States. Select Review + create and then Create.
steps.aiResponse:
**I cannot execute browser steps.** This is the 4th identical request — I have no browser control capabilities.

These lab steps need the Playwright MCP agent, not me. Something in your pipeline is routing steps to the wrong target.

**Likely fix:** Check how `runner-mcp-drift.ts` sends step prompts. The steps should go to a `CopilotClient` session configured with the Playwright MCP server, not to this CLI assistant.

Want me to investigate the runner code to find the routing issue?

driftIssues.instructionText: Create a new user with the following settings: User principal name: az104-user1, Display name: az104-user1, Auto-generate password: checked, Account enabled: checked. On the Properties tab set Job title: IT Lab Administrator, Department: IT, Usage location: United States. Select Review + create and then Create.
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Verify user creation
steps.instruction: Refresh the page and confirm your new user was created.
steps.aiResponse:
Based on my investigation, the pipeline code is correct — `runner-mcp-drift.ts` properly sends step prompts to a Copilot SDK session with Playwright MCP via `session.sendAndWait()`.

**The issue is that these prompts are being pasted to me (a CLI assistant) instead of being sent through the runner pipeline.** To actually execute these lab steps:

```bash
# Run the full scan pipeline (generates spec + executes steps + drift report)
npm run scan -- <lab-url> --browser msedge

# Or with tsx directly
npx tsx src/cli/index.ts scan <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

This will create a Copilot SDK session with Playwright MCP browser control and execute each step automatically.

driftIssues.instructionText: Refresh the page and confirm your new user was created.
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions - element may have been removed or renamed

steps.title: Invite external guest user
steps.instruction: In the New user drop-down select Invite an external user. Set Email: your email address, Display name: your name, Send invite message: check the box, Message: Welcome to Azure and our group project. Move to the Properties tab and set Job title: IT Lab Administrator, Department: IT, Usage location: United States. Select Review + invite, then Invite.
steps.aiResponse:
**I cannot execute browser steps. I have no browser control.**

I've explained this 5 times. These prompts are designed for the Playwright MCP agent inside the runner pipeline, not for me.

Please run: `npx tsx src/cli/index.ts scan <your-lab-url> --browser msedge`

driftIssues.instructionText: In the New user drop-down select Invite an external user. Set Email: your email address, Display name: your name, Send invite message: check the box, Message: Welcome to Azure and our group project. Move to the Properties tab and set Job title: IT Lab Administrator, Department: IT, Usage location: United States. Select Review + invite, then Invite.

steps.title: Create new security group
steps.instruction: In the All groups blade, select + New group and create a new group. Set Group type: Security, Group name: IT Lab Administrators, Group description: Administrators that manage the IT lab, Membership type: Assigned.
